question: When will the front-month WTI crude oil price return to our defined normalization threshold (WTI ≤ $68.26/bbl)?
key_assumptions: WTI price follows Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reversion process (log-normal)
Event fires when WTI falls_below τ
Calibration uses full 19-year history (2007–2026) to capture regime diversity
key_uncertainties: Mean-reversion assumption: current elevation may persist longer if structural (geopolitical supply disruption)
Regime stationarity: calibrated dynamics from past regimes may not apply to future shocks
Threshold definition: sensitivity to $68.26 choice (2025 75th pct; could use other baseline)
Short-term volatility clustering not fully captured by constant-vol OU (SV variant available)
